reforms.description: Norway's government has increased spending on research and education as part of its fiscal policy priorities, aiming to enhance public-sector efficiency and support economic growth. Additionally, the government is promoting entrepreneurial and STEM skills to enhance innovation and technology issues, alongside encouraging Technology Transfer Offices in universities.
reforms.component_name: Increased R&D Funding and Skills Promotion
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for research and education is expected to enhance public-sector efficiency and support economic growth, while promoting skills development fosters innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The increase in spending has been enacted as part of the government's fiscal policy priorities.
reforms.importance_rationale: The increase in spending on research and education represents a significant structural change in Norway's innovation policy.
reforms.source_quote: Address innovation and technology issues, including through: promotion of entrepreneurial skills and STEM skills, encouraging Technology Transfer Offices in universities.

reforms.description: Norway's Skattefunn tax break on R&D activity is a key element of its innovation policy, aimed at increasing R&D expenditures by providing tax credits to firms. A recent review indicates that for every NOK 1 of tax credit, R&D expenditures increase by more than NOK 2, highlighting its effectiveness.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credit incentivizes firms to increase their R&D expenditures, thereby fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The review of the tax break indicates its effectiveness, but it has not yet been fully implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While effective, the tax break is an extension of existing policy rather than a structural overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: A recent review of Skattefunn... estimates that R&D expenditures increases by more than NOK 2 for every 1 NOK of R&D tax credit.

reforms.description: The Norwegian government is establishing programs for training that target sectors and industries with the greatest need for upskilling or reskilling, alongside funding for short, flexible courses in advanced digital skills.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Targeted training programs are expected to enhance the skill set of the workforce, thereby supporting innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced the establishment of these programs, but they are not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of targeted training programs represents a significant investment in human capital development.
reforms.source_quote: The government will present a white paper on a skills reform to the Parliament in 2020.
